spec_match.rationale: Renders the same scene at different exposures into a float16 canvas — exactly the photography-style workflow the wider gamut enables.

interactive.rationale: Two input sliders drive the exposure and re-render the float16 canvas live.

edge_cases.rationale: Single-canvas demo without a unorm8 baseline next to it — hard to see the gain. Missing extreme-exposure clipping behaviour.

design_system.rationale: All variables, canvas drawing uses arbitrary colours which is expected content.

openQuestions.title: Add a side-by-side unorm8 baseline
openQuestions.detail: Without the unorm8 lane the float16 result looks like any other canvas. Render the same scene at the same exposure into both and let the user A/B them.

openQuestions.title: Show the clipping behaviour at +6 EV
openQuestions.detail: At extreme exposures float16 still saturates but unorm8 clips at +0.5 EV. Annotate where the two diverge so authors see the practical limit.

summary: Useful real-world workflow demo; a side-by-side unorm8 lane would make the float16 advantage immediately legible.
